metadata.context: Mock library for the AWS SDK, used in JS/TS test suites.

top_gaps.title: API Versioning Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No formal versioning strategy for the library's public API
top_gaps.impact: Breaking changes in exports could affect downstream consumers without warning

top_gaps.title: Database Engine Version and EOL
top_gaps.gap: AWS SDK dependency version range uses caret ranges without explicit pinning; Node.js 18 approaching EOL
top_gaps.impact: Implicit dependency resolution may introduce unexpected breaking changes

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: No input validation on public API functions; setSDK() uses require() with user-provided path
top_gaps.impact: Potential for arbitrary module loading if misused in non-test environments

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: All logic in a single 664-line file rather than decomposed into internal modules
top_gaps.impact: Reduced maintainability as the library grows

top_gaps.title: Service Discovery
top_gaps.gap: Default registry limited to 10 AWS services; no auto-discovery of installed @aws-sdk/client-* packages
top_gaps.impact: Consumers must manually configure non-default services

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and inline JSDoc comments
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent can index the library's documentation and source code to answer developer questions about mock usage patterns, API behavior, and troubleshooting
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate structured API documentation (e.g., TypeDoc output) for richer indexing

findings.title: Single-file module structure limits maintainability
findings.description: All core logic resides in a single 664-line file (src/index.ts) rather than being decomposed into focused internal modules for registry management, service stubbing, and method mocking.
findings.gap: No internal module decomposition. All logic in one file makes navigation, testing individual concerns, and parallel development harder.
findings.recommendation: Consider internal decomposition of src/index.ts into focused modules (e.g., registry.ts, service-mocker.ts, method-mocker.ts) for maintainability without changing the public API.

findings.title: No formal API versioning strategy
findings.description: The library uses npm semver (v6.2.2) but has no CHANGELOG, no deprecation annotations, and README still references AWS SDK v2 patterns despite being rewritten for v3.
findings.gap: No CHANGELOG tracking breaking changes. README content is partially outdated. No deprecation warnings for APIs that may change.
findings.recommendation: Add a CHANGELOG.md documenting breaking changes per version. Add JSDoc @deprecated annotations for legacy APIs. Update README to accurately reflect v3-only status.

findings.title: Limited default client registry
findings.description: The default registry covers only 10 AWS services. Consumers must manually register other services via setSDKInstance(). No auto-discovery of installed @aws-sdk/client-* packages.
findings.gap: Default registry limited to 10 services; no auto-discovery mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Consider implementing automatic discovery of installed @aws-sdk/client-* packages to reduce manual registry configuration for consumers.

findings.title: No structured documentation generation pipeline
findings.description: Documentation is manually maintained markdown files (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md) with JSDoc comments in source. No automated TypeDoc or similar generation pipeline exists.
findings.gap: No automated documentation generation or structured documentation pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add TypeDoc generation to the build pipeline to produce structured API documentation from JSDoc comments.

findings.title: Internal state not encapsulated
findings.description: The services object and _clientRegistry are module-level variables with no encapsulation boundary. All access flows through public API functions, but direct mutation is technically possible.
findings.gap: Module-level mutable state without encapsulation boundary.
findings.recommendation: Consider encapsulating services and _clientRegistry in a class or closure to enforce access only through the public API.

findings.title: Dependency version ranges and Node.js EOL risk
findings.description: All 11 AWS SDK dependencies use caret ranges (^3.750.0) allowing minor version drift. Node.js 18 engine minimum is approaching/past EOL (April 2025). CI matrix includes non-LTS Node 21.
findings.gap: Caret ranges allow drift. Node.js 18 approaching EOL. No documented version update procedure.
findings.recommendation: Update minimum Node.js engine to >=20 (current LTS). Add Node.js 22 to CI matrix and remove 21 (non-LTS). Document version update procedure for AWS SDK dependencies.

findings.title: Hard-coded service registry acts as implicit schema
findings.description: The defaultClientRegistry hard-codes 10 AWS service mappings that consumers depend on. Adding new services requires source code changes to the library.
findings.gap: Hard-coded registry creates maintenance burden when new AWS services are needed.
findings.recommendation: Consider making the default registry configurable or auto-discovered rather than hard-coded.

findings.title: No input validation on public API functions
findings.description: setSDK() uses require(path) with a user-provided path without validation. setSDKInstance() accepts any object without type checking. While this is a test utility, lack of validation could enable loading arbitrary modules.
findings.gap: No input validation on setSDK() path parameter or setSDKInstance() input.
findings.recommendation: Add input validation to setSDK() to restrict the path parameter. Document that setSDK() should only be used with trusted paths in test environments.

findings.title: No library-level audit logging
findings.description: GitHub Actions CI provides build/test audit logging. npm registry logs publish events. The library itself uses only console.log for error cases, not structured audit events.
findings.gap: No library-level structured audit logging beyond console.log in restore functions.
findings.recommendation: For a library, explicit audit logging is not required. Existing CI/CD and npm registry audit trails are sufficient.

findings.title: No package signing or provenance attestation
findings.description: The library has no data-at-rest surface. npm handles package integrity via SHA checksums. No explicit npm package provenance or signing is configured.
findings.gap: No npm package provenance attestation beyond built-in checksums.
findings.recommendation: Consider enabling npm package provenance via GitHub Actions for supply chain attestation.

findings.title: No CODEOWNERS file for ownership governance
findings.description: The library relies on npm and GitHub OAuth for identity. No CODEOWNERS file defines code review ownership for critical paths.
findings.gap: No CODEOWNERS file. Branch protection rules not visible in repository.
findings.recommendation: Add a CODEOWNERS file for src/ and test/ paths. Ensure branch protection requires review approval.

findings.title: No documented secret rotation policy
findings.description: GitHub Actions secrets are used for CODECOV_TOKEN. No plaintext credentials in source. However, no rotation policy is documented.
findings.gap: No documented rotation policy for the Codecov token.
findings.recommendation: Document secret rotation procedures. Consider short-lived OIDC tokens where supported.

findings.title: No npm audit step in CI pipeline
findings.description: Dependabot is configured for weekly dependency updates. CI tests across 3 Node versions and 3 OSes. No explicit npm audit step to block builds on vulnerabilities.
findings.gap: No npm audit in CI. Dependabot handles updates but does not block on critical vulnerabilities.
findings.recommendation: Add npm audit --audit-level=high to CI workflow to fail builds on high-severity vulnerabilities.

findings.title: No SAST in CI pipeline
findings.description: Dependabot is configured for dependency scanning. ESLint is configured but not run in CI (lint script runs only tsc). No SAST tool (CodeQL, Semgrep) is integrated. Snyk badge suggests external monitoring.
findings.gap: No SAST tool in CI. ESLint configured but not executed in pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add GitHub CodeQL or Semgrep to CI. Fix lint script to include ESLint alongside TypeScript compilation.

findings.title: No trace context consideration in mocked responses
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry instrumentation. Mocked AWS calls do not propagate trace context. This is expected behavior for mocks but undocumented.
findings.gap: No explicit consideration of trace context propagation in mocked responses.
findings.recommendation: Document that mocked calls do not propagate trace context so consumers know to use real clients for tracing tests.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires a deployable workload.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and there is no deployable workload to containerize.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 3 (no stored procedures); no commercial DB engines detected. Library has no database dependency.

triggering_questions.note: DATA-Q4 = 3 meets threshold; no commercial DB engines detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. Library has no database dependency.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires database infrastructure.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires data processing workloads.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — repo_type is 'library' and the pathway requires deployment pipelines.

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context describes a mock library for testing — no AI/agent/LLM signal terms found.
